# Expected files after /task runs
#
# /task is a thin router — it produces NO artifacts of its own. The target
# skill it invokes produces the actual output. This file documents the
# expected outputs per scenario for manual verification.

# Scenario A — tech debt → /refactor
# The router delegates to /refactor, which produces a diff/patch.
# Expected output: whatever /refactor produces (refactor-diff.patch
# with before/after and smell note — see fixture-07-daily-work-skills).

# Scenario B — direct /perf phrasing
# The router delegates to /perf. Expected output: perf-report.md
# (see fixture-07-daily-work-skills).

# Scenario C — chain refactor → test → review
# Three sub-reports, one per target skill:
#   refactor-diff.patch    (from /refactor)
#   tests/test_*.py        (from /test)
#   review-report.md       (from /review)

# Scenario D — new project redirect
# No /task artifacts. /project is invoked and produces its own outputs
# (STRATEGIC_PLAN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, etc. — see fixture-01-saas-clinic).

# /task router's own output
# The router adds ONE line to each scenario's final message indicating
# the routing decision. No standalone file, just a chat-level annotation.
